target.title: fix(cli): accept parent options placed after lazy subcommands (#55563 regression 1) [AI-assisted]
target.body:
> [AI-assisted] This PR was generated using Claude Code.

## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw browser tabs --browser-profile remote` (parent option placed *after* the subcommand) fails with `error: unknown option '--browser-profile'`. The pre-v2026.3.24 syntax `openclaw browser --browser-profile remote tabs` still works, so this is a silent regression of the option-ordering flexibility that existing WSL2/CDP documentation relies on. Regression 1 of #55563.
- **Why it matters now:** Every user following the existing browser/CDP troubleshooting docs gets an error that looks like a config problem, not a CLI syntax change. Reported by a contributor whose merged PR #39407 still documents the old form.
- **Intended outcome:** A parent option placed after a lazy subcommand is accepted again, matching the pre-lazy behavior and the existing root-option normalization (`--no-color`, `--log-level` after subcommands).
- **Out of scope:** Regression 2 of #55563 (gateway cycling after `openclaw doctor --fix`) is a separate code path already tracked by open PR #94148; this PR does not touch `doctor`.
- **Success look:** `openclaw browser tabs --browser-profile remote` parses and reaches the `tabs` action with `browserProfile === "remote"`, identical to `openclaw browser --browser-profile remote tabs`.
- **Reviewer focus:** `hoistParentOptionsBeforeSubcommand` in `src/cli/program/helpers.ts` and its single call site in `src/cli/program/action-reparse.ts`. The hoist only moves tokens that match a parent/ancestor command's own option flags from after the subcommand to before it; it is a no-op when parent options already precede the subcommand, so working invocations are unchanged.

## Linked context

Addresses Regression 1 of #55563 (the issue bundles two regressions; only the browser CLI syntax break is fixed here).

Related #55563 (Regression 2 — `doctor --fix` gateway cycling — tracked separately by #94148). Also related to the prior root-option-after-subcommand fixes #93455 (`--log-level`) and the `--no-color` normalization, which established the argv-normalization pattern this extends to parent-command options.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw browser tabs --browser-profile remote` rejected `--browser-profile` as an unknown option because the lazy reparse replays the original argv verbatim and the real `tabs` subcommand does not define (or allow) the parent's `--browser-profile`.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.2.2, OpenClaw built from source on branch `fix/vuln-55563`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the real CLI `node ./openclaw.mjs browser tabs --browser-profile remote` and the lazy-reparse integration test in `extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts` (real Commander parse + real lazy reparse; only the browser network modules are stubbed, the option-parsing path under fix is real).
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):
```
$ node ./openclaw.mjs browser tabs --browser-profile remote   (real CLI, fix applied)
(no "unknown option" error — parse accepts --browser-profile after the subcommand;
 command proceeds into the tabs action. No browser gateway is running in this env,
 so it does not list tabs, but the option-ordering regression is resolved.)

$ pnpm test -- extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts   (fix applied)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
  accepts --browser-profile placed after the lazy subcommand (#55563 regression 1)
    -> tabsAction called once; browser parent opts browserProfile === "remote"
```
- Observed result after fix: The parent option `--browser-profile remote` placed after the `tabs` subcommand is accepted; the `tabs` action runs with `browserProfile === "remote"`, identical to placing the option before the subcommand. A bare `--browser-profile` with no following value (or followed by another flag like `--json`) is left in place, so Commander still reports the missing-value/unknown-option error and the `tabs` action does not run — the bare flag is never hoisted alone (which would let Commander consume the next token as the value).
```
$ node ./openclaw.mjs browser tabs --browser-profile   (real CLI, fix applied)
error: unknown option '--browser-profile'
(missing-value path preserved: bare parent value option is NOT hoisted alone,
 so the subcommand name is never swallowed as the option value)

$ pnpm test -- src/cli/program/helpers.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts   (fix applied)
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  57 passed (57)
  ✓ does not hoist a bare value option with no following value
  ✓ does not hoist a value option followed by another flag
  ✓ does not hoist an empty --flag= form
  ✓ preserves the missing-value error for a bare --browser-profile after the subcommand (#55563 regression 1)
  ✓ preserves the missing-value error when --browser-profile is followed by another flag (#55563 regression 1)
```
- What was not tested: macOS/Linux, non-browser lazy command trees with deeply nested subcommands beyond the two-level `browser tab new` shape, and the actual tab listing against a live browser gateway (no browser running in this environment).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The before/after contrast is captured through the real lazy-reparse path exercised by the integration test (real Commander + real reparse + real hoist; browser network modules stubbed) and corroborated by the real `openclaw` CLI accepting the option. A live browser gateway was not available locally.
- Before evidence:
```
$ pnpm test -- extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts   (fix reverted, HEAD~1)
error: unknown option '--browser-profile'
 |extension-browser| extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts (8 tests | 1 failed)
  x accepts --browser-profile placed after the lazy subcommand (#55563 regression 1)
Error: process.exit unexpectedly called with "1"
  Command.error node_modules/commander/lib/command.js:1957:10
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 7 passed (8)
```

## Tests and validation

- Commands run: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` (changed files clean), `pnpm tsgo` (exit 0), `pnpm exec oxlint --type-aware` on changed files (exit 0), `pnpm build` (exit 0, including `write-cli-startup-metadata` postbuild), `pnpm test -- src/cli/program/helpers.test.ts src/cli/program/action-reparse.test.ts` (47 + 6 pass), `pnpm test -- extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts` (10 pass).
- Regression coverage added: `hoistParentOptionsBeforeSubcommand` unit tests (`helpers.test.ts`, 11 cases: value/boolean hoist, `--flag=value`, no-op when already before, subcommand-own-option preserved, root-option skip, root-parent no-op, subcommand-not-found no-op, plus 3 missing-value preservation cases — bare value option, value option followed by another flag, empty `--flag=` form); an `action-reparse.test.ts` case asserting the hoisted argv is re-parsed; and `browser-cli.lazy.test.ts` cases asserting `browser tabs --browser-profile remote` reaches the `tabs` action with `browserProfile === "remote"` (fails on `HEAD~1` with `unknown option '--browser-profile'`) and that bare `browser tabs --browser-profile` / `browser tabs --browser-profile --json` preserve the missing-value error (the `tabs` action does not run).
- What failed before this fix: `openclaw browser tabs --browser-profile remote` exited with `error: unknown option '--browser-profile'`.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — a previously-rejected option placement (`browser tabs --browser-profile remote`) is now accepted, restoring pre-v2026.3.24 behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No. No CODEOWNERS secops paths are touched.

What is the highest-risk area? The shared lazy-reparse path (`reparseProgramFromActionArgs`) is used by every lazy CLI command, so a hoist bug could affect other commands.

How is that risk mitigated? The hoist is strictly conservative: it only relocates tokens that match a parent/ancestor command's own option flags (value vs boolean resolved from Commander `Option.required`/`optional`), excludes options the subcommand itself defines, stops at `--`, and returns argv unchanged when no parent option appears after the subcommand. Working invocations (parent options before the subcommand) are untouched (hoisted array is empty, early return). New unit + integration tests cover the no-op and hoist paths; existing lazy-command tests are unchanged by the fix (pre-existing Windows-only failures in `run-main.exit`/`update-cli` fail identically on clean `main`).

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and CI run.

Addressed in the follow-up push (commit `83bf4222dc`): the ClawSweeper/Codex P2 finding — `consumeHoistableOption` no longer hoists a parent value option that has no following value. A bare `--browser-profile` (or `--browser-profile --json`, or `--browser-profile=`) is left in place, preserving Commander's missing-value error instead of swallowing the next token as the option value. Added 3 `helpers.test.ts` and 2 `browser-cli.lazy.test.ts` regression cases for the missing-value paths; reran the focused lazy CLI tests, oxlint on touched files, and `git diff --check` (all clean).

Still waiting on: maintainer review; the heavy CI shards (which require maintainer `Authorize workflow actor` approval for first-time contributor workflow runs) and the automated Real behavior proof / ClawSweeper re-review.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: ClawSweeper/Codex P2 finding "Preserve missing parent option errors" (`src/cli/program/helpers.ts:172`) — fixed and covered by regression tests as described above.
target.author: ml12580
